name: Adobe Experience Manager MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) — Adobe's enterprise content management platform for digital experiences. Enables AI agents to manage AEM content, assets, pages, components, and workflows — supporting AI-driven content authoring, asset management, and digital experience delivery through AEM's REST APIs.
use_cases: Managing AEM content pages and components from AI content authoring agents
Querying and organizing AEM digital assets from AI asset management agents
Triggering AEM workflows and publishing processes from content automation agents
Integrating AEM content management into AI-driven digital experience workflows
not_for: Teams not using Adobe Experience Manager (WordPress, Drupal, Contentful users)
Small business websites (AEM is enterprise-scale, expensive CMS)
best_when: An enterprise digital marketing team using Adobe AEM wants AI agents to automate content creation, asset management, and publishing workflows within their AEM environment.
avoid_when: You use other CMS platforms. AEM is an enterprise-only product with significant licensing costs — not for SMBs.

auth.notes: AEM user credentials or OAuth token via Adobe IMS. Service credentials for API access. Requires AEM instance with API enabled.

pricing.free_tier_limits: AEM is enterprise-only — no free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Adobe AEM licensing: typically $250K+/year for enterprise
pricing.notes: AEM is a premium enterprise CMS. Community MCP implementation (easingthemes). MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: Bounded by AEM instance performance and licensing

known_agent_gotchas: AEM API complexity is significant — agents need AEM-specific knowledge (Sling, JCR, OSGi concepts)
AEM version differences (AEM 6.5 vs AEM as a Cloud Service) affect API behavior
Content publishing is irreversible without rollback — agents should operate on staging first
Community MCP implementation — not official Adobe product; verify AEM API coverage
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise content platform. Adobe IMS OAuth for AEM Cloud Service. Content publishing affects production sites — use staging environments. SOC2/ISO27001 compliant.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise CMS. Adobe IMS auth. SOC2/ISO27001. Content publishing to production — use staging first.
